steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The first clause identifies the shesep and heqat as ancient Egyptian units of measurement. The next idea begins with the modifier "Used to record length and volume, respectively" and leads into a new independent clause about those units falling out of use.

steps.title: Choose a sentence boundary
steps.content:
• Choice A: "And used" fuses the modifier awkwardly into the first clause instead of creating a clear boundary.
  • Choice C: A comma leaves two independent clauses joined incorrectly.
  • Choice D: No punctuation creates a run-on between the measurement statement and the later claim.
The answer is B.
